---
title: "Introduction to Graph Theory"
date: "2023-06-24"
categories: [graph theory]
format:
  html: 
    code-fold: false
---




A *graph* is a way of specifying relationships among a collection of items. A graph consists of a set of objects, called *vertices*, or sometimes we call it nodes in general, with certain pairs of these objects connected by links called *edges*. We use $G$ to denote any graph we want to discuss, or specifically, $G = (V,E)$, where $V$ represents vertices of a graph and $E$ edges, which is an ordered pair.
